qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF in Workflow Import from URL Feature
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF via GET /workflows/from-url due to missing URL validation in workflows.controller.ts:307-343. The advisory describes stored XSS via script injection in node-rendered pages (Form Trigger, Chat Trigger, Send & Wait, Webhook, Chat nodes). The root cause (missing URL allowlist vs missing output sanitization), vulnerability class (SSRF vs XSS), and vulnerable code paths are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes stored XSS (CWE-79/CWE-80) in n8n where authenticated users inject arbitrary scripts into pages rendered by Form Trigger, Chat Trigger, Send & Wait, Webhook, and Chat nodes. The sole qualifying finding describes SSRF via a workflow import URL feature. The root cause, vulnerability class, and vulnerable code are entirely different from the advisory-described XSS.
evidence: Advisory root cause: missing output sanitization in node-rendered pages allowing script injection (CWE-79/CWE-80)
Advisory affected nodes: Form Trigger, Chat Trigger, Send & Wait, Webhook, Chat Node
Finding 'ssrf-workflow-import': SSRF via GET /workflows/from-url (workflows.controller.ts:307-343) - different root cause, different vulnerability class, different sink
